```python
from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        apps.get_app_config('your_app_name').models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        apps.get_app_config('your_app_name').models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```

from django import forms

from django.forms import ModelForm

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):

        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Replace with your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```

```python
from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # BUG: this behavior is incorrect but currently happens due to the bug
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should be present when the bug occurs
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should be present when the bug occurs

```python
from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should be present when it should not be
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):

        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues

    def setUp(self):

        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # BUG: this behavior is incorrect but currently happens due to the bug
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```

```python
from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_includes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field incorrectly renders a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the presence of the blank option
        self.assertIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should be present when the bug occurs
```

from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False

```python
from django import forms
from django.test import SimpleTestCase
from django.forms import ModelForm
from django.db import models
from django.apps import apps

# Define a minimal BatchData model for testing purposes
class BatchData(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class TestRun(models.Model):
    data_file = models.ForeignKey(BatchData, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=False)

class TestRunForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = TestRun
        fields = ['data_file']
        widgets = {'data_file': forms.RadioSelect()}

class TestRunFormTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        super().setUpClass()
        # Register the BatchData model to avoid app_label issues
        cls.app_label = 'test_app'  # Ensure this matches your actual app label
        apps.get_app_config(cls.app_label).models['BatchData'] = BatchData

    def setUp(self):
        # Create a BatchData instance to use in the form
        self.batch_data_instance = BatchData.objects.create(name="First Data File")

    def test_radio_select_excludes_blank_option_when_required(self):
        """
        Test that the RadioSelect widget for the data_file field does not render a blank option
        when blank=False is set on the model, exposing the bug.
        """
        # Instantiate the form without initial data
        form = TestRunForm()
        rendered_form = form.as_p()  # Render the form to HTML

        # Check for the absence of the blank option
        self.assertNotIn('---------', rendered_form)  # This should not be present when blank=False
```
